so this is the difference in syntax you have to remember you don't need to run brackets but if you want to run two upper case and two lower case then you need to run brackets so this is one thing now what we will do we will see how you can slice a string, now if i want that i get all characters from 2 to 4 and 4 is not included then i can use .slice method, this is something that you are going to use a lot so if i say b.slice 1,5 or 1,4 then what will happen, you mark it from 0 s is 0, h is 1 this is 2, this is 3 this is 4, now 4 is not included so what will happen hiv will be the output so now see this is the output if i make it 1 to 5 then hiv will be there so what will happen, 1 is included and 5 is not included so this thing you should know now here you see if i give only one number then it will take the whole string till end, if i say .slice and not 1,5 and give only 1 then what will it do, it will give all characters from 1 1 here is index so from 1 index to 5 index it will take all characters hivm as you have seen now if i write name is equal to harry bhai and then i want to replace bhai with bhau, so i can do or if i want here console.log and i say b. and replace and i want to replace suppose sh and suppose i want to replace with 77 suppose i want to replace with 77, so you see print will be 77ivm so whatever i want to replace it will be replaced now i will show you one thing, if there are two occurrences then what will happen so if there are two occurrences then first occurrence will be replaced you have to remember this, first occurrence will be replaced, both occurrences are not replaced so you have to remember this after this you can concatenate two strings, you can say make a new string which is name1 and name2 concat, you can even add more strings in this, so here you see if i write console.log and i say b. concat and i say a so i will do this and run, so you see shivam sh harry is written, because a was our harry so both are concatenated suppose i want to concatenate with a and i want to write ashwarya so if i write ashwarya so you see here ashwarya is added and whatever you want to add it will be added so if you want to add anyone else, then it is so easy, you can do suppose i do rahul and priya and now you see shivam sh harry ashwarya rahul and priya are added so in this way you concatenate and give as many strings as you want and they will all be concatenated to do this you can use plus but according to me it is more convenient when you want to make a new string or you can use plus it also concatenates, so i have written here in notes, we can even use plus operator, now if you want to remove white spaces from any string so you will make a new string and write name.trim, so it will remove white spaces so this is one thing you have to remember now a very important thing that the string is immutable, means whatever i have told you in that i am making a new string, you see same string will not change, means after doing all this if i console.log and do b again, so it will remain the same as my initial shivam if i remove it, so shivam will remain the same, so you see and if i do sh here, so sh will also come so once you have made a string and the allocation is done in memory after that you can not change that string again, you can make a new string by using this kind of functions this is a new string, b.to uppercase means make a new string which is b's uppercase, b.lowercase means to lowercase means you make a lowercase string of b but you can not change b so this is called immutability you can not change the
